cards.notes: 大学生 (daigakusei) = college student. 中学生 (chuugakusei) = middle school student.
Used for teachers, doctors, lawyers, and other respected professionals.
Group 2 (ichidan) verb. 食べます (tabemasu) = polite form. 食べた (tabeta) = past tense.
Group 1 (godan) verb. 飲みます (nomimasu) = polite form. お水を飲む = drink water.
Irregular te-form: 行って (itte). 学校に行く = go to school. Direction particle: に or へ.
Irregular verb. 来ます (kimasu), 来た (kita), 来て (kite). 日本に来る = come to Japan.
Irregular verb. します (shimasu). Creates verbs from nouns: 勉強する (benkyou suru) = to study.
い-adjective. 大きくない = not big. 大きかった = was big.
い-adjective. Opposite of 大きい. 小さいねこ = small cat.
い-adjective. 新しいくるま = new car. Opposite: 古い (ふるい) = old.
Two meanings depending on context. 高いビル = tall building. 高いくつ = expensive shoes.
い-adjective. Opposite of 高い (expensive). 安くておいしい = cheap and delicious.
今日の天気 = today's weather. いい天気ですね = Nice weather, isn't it?
三時間 (san-jikan) = 3 hours. 時間がない = don't have time.
電話する = to make a phone call. 電話番号 = phone number.
友達になる = to become friends. 親友 (しんゆう) = close/best friend.
ご家族 (go-kazoku) = someone else's family (polite). 家族は四人です = My family has 4 people.
毎朝 (maiasa) = every morning. 毎週 (maishuu) = every week. 毎月 (maitsuki) = every month.
すし が 好きです = I like sushi. 大好き (daisuki) = love/really like.
日本語がわかります = I understand Japanese. わかりません = I don't understand.
